metadata.purpose: Tier 1 state sports betting revenue reporting research

NY.launch_date_notes: Mobile/online launched Jan 8, 2022. Retail at commercial casinos predates this.
NY.requires_js_notes: Page loads fine but download links rendered via JS. Playwright needed for full capture.
NY.operator_notes: 9 licensed mobile sports wagering operators. Also a 'Statewide Sports Wagering' aggregated report. theScore Bet = Penn Entertainment platform. Rush Street Interactive = BetRivers brand.
NY.sport_breakdown_notes: NY does NOT break down by sport. Reports are aggregate handle/GGR per operator.
NY.retail_online_split_notes: NY only has mobile/online sports betting. No retail sportsbooks under the mobile license structure. Retail is at commercial casinos separately under different reporting.

data_fields.name: Mobile Sports Wagering Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered on mobile sports betting

data_fields.name: Gross Gaming Revenue (GGR)
data_fields.description: Revenue after payouts to bettors

data_fields.name: Promotional Credits
data_fields.description: Value of promotional/bonus credits used

data_fields.name: Net Revenue
data_fields.description: GGR minus promotional credits

data_fields.name: Prior Period Adjustments
data_fields.description: Adjustments from previous reporting periods

NY.data_fields_notes: Weekly reports aggregate Mon-Sun. Monthly reports aggregate calendar month. Reports available per-operator and statewide.
NY.ggr_definition: Sports wagering gross gaming revenue is reported on a cash basis. Wagers on future events are taxed as current revenue and payouts for winning wagers are recognized in the period redeemed.
NY.ggr_formula: GGR = Total Handle - Payouts to Bettors
NY.promo_deduction: Promotional credits are reported but their deductibility from taxable revenue is limited. Net Revenue = GGR - Promotional Credits.
NY.retail_tax_rate: N/A (different license structure for retail at commercial casinos, typically 10%)
NY.tax_notes: 51% is the highest state tax rate in the US for online sports betting. Applied to mobile sports wagering GGR.
NY.scraping_approach: Page requires JS to render download links. Use Playwright to navigate to gaming.ny.gov/revenue-reports, extract download URLs for each operator's weekly/monthly PDF and Excel reports. Direct curl download is blocked (403). Need to use browser automation. Download pattern: /[operator]-[frequency]-report-[format]. Statewide aggregated reports also available.
NY.anti_scraping: 403 on direct curl requests to file URLs. Requires browser-like user-agent or full browser automation.
NY.sample_files_notes: Direct curl downloads returned 403 errors (24KB error pages). Playwright or authenticated session needed.
NY.notes: NY is the largest US sports betting market by revenue (~$2.5B GGR in 2025). 51% tax rate makes it the most lucrative for state coffers. Only mobile/online, no retail under mobile license. FanDuel and DraftKings dominate ~70% of market.
NY.log: WebFetch successful for page structure. WebSearch provided tax/GGR details. Playwright screenshot captured. Sample file download failed due to 403.

IL.launch_date_notes: In-person sports wagering went live March 9, 2020. Online wagering began June 18, 2020.
IL.reporting_frequency_notes: Revenue figures are not due to IGB until the last day of the month following the month the revenue was earned.
IL.requires_js_notes: Page loads but Playwright timed out on networkidle (loaded on domcontentloaded). Some JS-dependent content.
IL.operator_notes: 10 online sportsbooks currently operating. Law allows up to 23 Master Licenses tied to land-based casinos plus 3 online-only licenses (26 total possible). bet365 launched March 16, 2025.
IL.sport_breakdown_notes: IL reports do not appear to break down by sport in the standard monthly reports.
IL.retail_online_split_notes: Reports distinguish between in-person (retail) and online sports wagering.

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered by patrons

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Receipts (AGR)
data_fields.description: Operator revenue after payouts

data_fields.name: Privilege Tax
data_fields.description: State tax paid by operators

IL.data_fields_notes: CSV files available for data manipulation. PDF provides formatted reports. Exact CSV column headers need to be verified from actual download.
IL.ggr_definition: Adjusted Gross Receipts (AGR) = Total Handle minus payouts to bettors. AGR and GGR are used interchangeably in Illinois.
IL.promo_deduction: Illinois does not allow promotional credit deductions from AGR for tax purposes.
IL.tax_notes: Graduated tax structure introduced in 2024: 20% on AGR up to $30M, 25% on $30M-$50M, 30% on $50M-$100M, 35% on $100M-$200M, 40% on $200M+. Additionally, a per-wager excise tax began July 2025: $0.25 per wager for first 20M bets, $0.50 per wager thereafter.
IL.scraping_approach: CSV files are available for direct download from igb.illinois.gov. Use Playwright to navigate to the reports page, find CSV download links, and download. CSV format is ideal for automated parsing. Page may have JS-rendered download links.
IL.anti_scraping: Page loads slowly (Playwright timeout on networkidle). May need longer timeouts or domcontentloaded strategy.
IL.sample_files_notes: No files downloaded in this session. CSV files available on the IGB website.
IL.notes: IL is a top-5 US market with ~$25.65B handle in 2025. Graduated tax + per-wager fee is unique. IGB also has a Monthly Receipts System (igbapps.illinois.gov/smr/) but requires login. CSV availability makes this relatively scraper-friendly once download URLs are identified.
IL.log: WebFetch successful for page structure. WebSearch provided tax/operator details. Playwright screenshot captured (with domcontentloaded). No sample files downloaded due to JS-rendered download links.

PA.launch_date_notes: Retail sports betting launched November 17, 2018 at Hollywood Casino. Online/mobile launched May 31, 2019.
PA.archive_depth: FY 2006/2007 through FY 2025/2026 (filterable by fiscal year on website)
PA.requires_js_notes: Direct download URLs work with curl. PDF and Excel files accessible via static URLs.
PA.operator_notes: 17 licensed facility operators. Reports use casino/facility name, not sportsbook brand name. Valley Forge Casino = FanDuel's primary PA skin. Hollywood Casinos = Penn Entertainment (ESPN Bet). Rivers = Rush Street Interactive (BetRivers). Harrah's = Caesars. Parx = Parx Casino brand.
PA.sport_breakdown_notes: PA reports do NOT break down by sport. Data is handle/revenue per facility.
PA.retail_online_split_notes: Each facility shows Total Sports Wagering, then Retail Sports Wagering and Online Sports Wagering separately with full detail.

data_fields.name: Handle*
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (* indicates handle definition footnote)

data_fields.name: Revenue
data_fields.description: Gross revenue before promotional credit deductions

data_fields.name: Promotional Credits
data_fields.description: Dollar value of promotional/bonus credits wagered

data_fields.name: Gross Revenue (Taxable)
data_fields.description: Revenue minus promotional credits (tax base)

data_fields.name: State Tax Due (34%)
data_fields.description: 34% state tax on taxable gross revenue

data_fields.name: Local Share Assessment (2%)
data_fields.description: 2% local share assessment on taxable gross revenue

PA.data_fields_notes: Monthly Excel report organized by facility with columns for each month (Jul-Jun fiscal year). Each facility has three sections: Total, Retail, and Online. Grand Total row at bottom. FY 2025/2026 total column included.
PA.ggr_definition: Gross Revenue (Taxable) = Revenue minus Promotional Credits. Revenue = Handle minus payouts. Promotional credits ARE deductible.
PA.ggr_formula: Taxable Gross Revenue = (Handle - Payouts) - Promotional Credits
PA.promo_deduction: Yes, promotional credits are fully deductible from gross revenue to arrive at taxable gross revenue.
PA.online_tax_rate: 34% state tax + 2% local share assessment = 36% effective
PA.retail_tax_rate: 34% state tax + 2% local share assessment = 36% effective
PA.tax_notes: 36% effective tax rate (34% state + 2% local) is the second-highest competitive commercial market rate in the US. Same rate for online and retail.
PA.scraping_approach: Direct download URLs for PDF and Excel files work via curl without authentication or JS. URL pattern: gamingcontrolboard.pa.gov/sites/default/files/{YYYY}-{MM}/FY{YY}-{YY}%20Monthly%20Sports%20Wagering%20Report%20Summary.{xlsx|pdf}. Excel format is ideal for automated parsing. Monthly updates on fiscal year basis.
PA.anti_scraping: None observed. Direct file downloads work.
PA.notes: PA is one of the easiest states to scrape. Direct Excel downloads, clear column headers, facility-level breakdowns with retail/online split. Fiscal year runs Jul-Jun. 17 facilities report. Over $8.7B online handle in 2025. Revenue spiked 118% YoY in Dec 2025 to $103.8M.
PA.log: WebFetch successful. WebSearch provided tax/revenue details. Both PDF and Excel downloaded successfully. PA PDF fully read and analyzed. Playwright screenshot captured. EASY scraping target.

NJ.regulatory_body: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement (DGE), under the Office of the Attorney General
NJ.url_notes: Main page returns 403 to WebFetch. Monthly reports accessible via alternate URL at nj.gov/oag/ge/docs/Financials/
NJ.launch_date_notes: Sports betting launched June 14, 2018 at Monmouth Park racetrack (first legal bet outside Nevada). Online launched shortly after.
NJ.file_formats_notes: Monthly press release PDFs with detailed tables. No Excel/CSV files identified for sports wagering specifically.
NJ.archive_depth: Since June 2018 launch. Reports organized by year in URL structure.
NJ.requires_js_notes: PDF files accessible via direct URL pattern. Main NJOAG page may block bots but PDFs on nj.gov subdomain are accessible.
NJ.operator_notes: NJ uses casino/racetrack licensee model. Each brand is tied to a physical property license. CIENJ = Caesars Interactive Entertainment New Jersey (covers Caesars, Harrah's, Tropicana brands). Reports separately detail Casino Hotels (9), Racetracks (Meadowlands, Monmouth Park), and online brands.
NJ.sport_breakdown_notes: Sports Wagering Statistics page shows 'Industry Completed Events Win and Handle Statistics Breakdown by Category' with Year-To-Date Completed Events Win, Handle, and Win%. This is aggregate industry level, NOT per-operator. Parlay is treated as its own category. Data is 'unaudited and subject to adjustment'.
NJ.retail_online_split_notes: Extensive retail vs online split. Main statistics table shows Online Sports Wagering and Retail Sports Wagering separately. Dedicated comparison pages for each: 'Online Sports Wagering Gross Revenue Comparison' and 'Retail Sports Wagering Gross Revenue Comparison' with per-brand breakdowns. Handle statistics also split Retail/Online.

data_fields.name: Online Sports Wagering
data_fields.description: Gross revenue from online/mobile sports betting

data_fields.name: Retail Sports Wagering
data_fields.description: Gross revenue from in-person sports betting

data_fields.name: Sports Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: Total of online + retail sports wagering gross revenue

data_fields.name: Monthly Handle (Retail)
data_fields.description: Total retail sports wagers placed in the month

data_fields.name: Monthly Handle (Online)
data_fields.description: Total online sports wagers placed in the month

data_fields.name: Total Handle
data_fields.description: Sum of retail and online handle

data_fields.name: Year-To-Date Completed Events Win
data_fields.description: Win by sport category for completed events YTD

data_fields.name: Year-To-Date Completed Events Handle
data_fields.description: Handle by sport category for completed events YTD

data_fields.name: Win %
data_fields.description: Win as percentage of handle for each sport category

data_fields.name: Casino Gross Revenue Tax (8%)
data_fields.description: 8% tax on casino gross revenue

data_fields.name: Internet Gaming Tax (19.75%)
data_fields.description: Tax on internet gaming gross revenue

data_fields.name: Retail Sports Wagering Tax (8.5%)
data_fields.description: Tax on retail sports wagering gross revenue

data_fields.name: Online Sports Wagering Tax (19.75%)
data_fields.description: Tax on online sports wagering gross revenue

data_fields.name: Investment Alternative Tax (1.25%)
data_fields.description: Additional tax on casino and internet gaming revenue

data_fields.name: Casino Sports Wagering Additional Tax (1.25%)
data_fields.description: Additional tax on casino sports wagering revenue

data_fields.name: Racetrack Sports Wagering Additional Tax (1.25%)
data_fields.description: Additional tax on racetrack sports wagering revenue

data_fields.name: % Change (YoY)
data_fields.description: Year-over-year percentage change

data_fields.name: Increase (Decrease) $
data_fields.description: Year-over-year dollar change

NJ.data_fields_notes: NJ provides the most comprehensive monthly report of any state. Multi-page PDF includes: main statistics summary, gross revenue tax breakdown by operator, casino win comparison, internet gaming win comparison, online sports wagering gross revenue comparison, retail sports wagering gross revenue comparison, and sports wagering statistics (by sport). All data includes current month, prior year month, YoY change, YTD, and prior YTD.
NJ.ggr_definition: Sports Wagering Gross Revenue = total amount wagered minus payouts to bettors. This is the 'win' for operators.
NJ.ggr_formula: Sports Wagering Gross Revenue = Handle - Payouts
NJ.promo_deduction: Limited. NJ Governor vetoed a bill that would have permitted broad sports-betting promo deductions. Operators can only deduct promotional credits exceeding $7.5M per month. The 8% Casino Gross Revenue Tax includes impact of eligible promotional gaming credits.
NJ.tax_notes: Tax rates changed mid-2025. Before July 1, 2025: online sports 13%, internet gaming 15%. After July 1, 2025: both increased to 19.75%. Additional 1.25% tax applies to both casino and racetrack sports wagering licensees. Retail remains 8.5%.
NJ.scraping_approach: Monthly PDF reports follow predictable URL pattern: https://www.nj.gov/oag/ge/docs/Financials/PressRelease{YYYY}/{MonthName}{YYYY}.pdf. Direct curl downloads work. PDF parsing needed (tabular data). No Excel/CSV available - need PDF table extraction (tabula/pdfplumber). Alternatively, scrape the monthly sports wagering tax returns page for more granular data.
NJ.anti_scraping: NJOAG main page blocks WebFetch (403). However, PDF files on nj.gov/oag/ge/docs/ subdomain are directly downloadable via curl without issues.
NJ.notes: NJ provides the richest data set of any state: operator-level breakdown for online + retail, sport-by-sport handle/win, tax information per operator. Key weakness: PDF-only format requires table extraction. NJ was the pioneer of legal sports betting (post-PASPA). $13.96B handle in 2025. Tax hike to 19.75% in mid-2025 was significant.
NJ.log: WebFetch returned 403 for main page. PDF downloaded successfully via curl. Full 8-page PDF analyzed with detailed operator, sport, tax data. WebSearch provided tax rate/GGR details. Playwright screenshot captured.

OH.url_notes: WebFetch returned 404 on first attempt but page loads in browser. PDF report hosted on separate CDN (dam.assets.ohio.gov).
OH.launch_date_notes: Sports betting launched at 12:01 AM ET on January 1, 2023.
OH.file_formats_notes: Multi-page PDF with summary page and detailed monthly tabs. Hosted on Ohio's DAM CDN. URL pattern: dam.assets.ohio.gov/image/upload/v{version}/casinocontrol.ohio.gov/revenue-reports/{YYYY}/Sports/{YYYY}_Sports_Gaming_Revenue_Report.pdf
OH.archive_depth: Since January 2023 launch. Annual PDF reports with monthly tabs.
OH.requires_js_notes: Main page may require JS. But PDF reports are on CDN and directly downloadable.
operators_online_type_a: HARD ROCK CINCINNATI - SEMINOLE HARD ROCK DIGITAL
HOLLYWOOD GAMING AT DAYTON RACEWAY - RSI OH, LLC
MIAMI VALLEY GAMING AND RACING - MVGBET
operators_retail_type_b: HARD ROCK CINCINNATI - SEMINOLE HARD ROCK DIGITAL
OH.operator_notes: OH uses 'Proprietor / Services Provider' naming combining venue name with brand. Type A = Online, Type B = Retail. Unique model ties licenses to sports teams (Browns, Guardians, Blue Jackets, Reds, FC Cincinnati) and casino venues. FC CINCINNATI - SUPERBOOK shows no activity.
OH.sport_breakdown_notes: OH does NOT break down by sport. Data is per-operator with online/retail split.
OH.retail_online_split_notes: Clear Type A (Online) vs Type B (Retail) split. Summary page shows Online Revenue, Retail Revenue, Total Revenue, and Total Taxable Revenue by month. Detailed monthly pages show full breakdown per operator for each type.

data_fields.name: Total Gross Receipts
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (handle)

data_fields.name: (-) Winnings Paid
data_fields.description: All cash and cash equivalents paid as winnings to sports gaming patrons

data_fields.name: (-) Voided Wagers
data_fields.description: The dollar amount of all voided wagers

data_fields.name: Promotional*
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered using promotional funds (reported but NOT deductible until 2027)

data_fields.name: Revenue
data_fields.description: Sports Gaming Receipts = Gross Receipts - Winnings Paid - Voided Wagers

data_fields.name: Taxable Revenue
data_fields.description: Revenue floored at zero (losses cannot be carried forward)

data_fields.name: Online Revenue (summary)
data_fields.description: Aggregate online revenue for the month

data_fields.name: Retail Revenue (summary)
data_fields.description: Aggregate retail revenue for the month

data_fields.name: Total Revenue (summary)
data_fields.description: Online + Retail revenue

data_fields.name: Total Taxable Revenue (summary)
data_fields.description: Taxable revenue (negatives zeroed out)

OH.data_fields_notes: OH provides the most granular revenue decomposition: full formula visible (Gross Receipts - Winnings - Voided = Revenue). Promotional credits shown but NOT currently deductible. Each monthly tab has separate Type A Online and Type B Retail sections with subtotals and grand total.
OH.ggr_definition: Sports Gaming Receipts per Rev.Code 5753.01(H): Gross Receipts minus Winnings Paid minus Voided Wagers minus eligible Promotional Gaming Credits. Currently, promotional credits are NOT deductible (deductibility begins January 1, 2027 at 10%, increasing to 20% on January 1, 2032).
OH.ggr_formula: Revenue = Total Gross Receipts - Winnings Paid - Voided Wagers (Promotional Credits NOT currently deducted but will be partially deductible starting 2027)
OH.promo_deduction: NOT deductible until January 1, 2027. After 2027: 10% of promotional credits deductible. After 2032: 20% of promotional credits deductible. Per Rev.Code 5753.01(H)(4)(a) and (b).
OH.tax_basis: Taxable Revenue (Revenue floored at zero; losses not carried forward)
OH.tax_notes: 20% flat tax on taxable revenue. Nearly all revenue goes to state general fund for schools. 2% funds problem gambling efforts. Negative revenue for an operator in a month is zeroed out for tax purposes.
OH.scraping_approach: PDF hosted on Ohio's CDN (dam.assets.ohio.gov) is directly downloadable via curl. Annual PDF with monthly tabs. URL pattern: dam.assets.ohio.gov/image/upload/v{version_id}/casinocontrol.ohio.gov/revenue-reports/{YYYY}/Sports/{YYYY}_Sports_Gaming_Revenue_Report.pdf. PDF table extraction needed (tabula/pdfplumber). One file per year with all months.
OH.anti_scraping: None observed on CDN file URLs. Direct download works.
OH.notes: OH provides exceptionally detailed data with full revenue formula visible. Unique features: sports team-tied licenses (Browns, Guardians, etc.), explicit promotional credit tracking, future promo deductibility schedule. $10.3B handle in 2025. FanDuel and DraftKings dominate. Revenue formula transparency is best-in-class.
OH.log: WebFetch returned 404 for main page. PDF downloaded successfully from CDN. Full 10-page PDF analyzed with 8 monthly tabs + summary + revenue notes. WebSearch provided tax/operator details. Playwright screenshot captured. EASY scraping target.

MI.url_notes: WebFetch returns 403. Page loads in Playwright browser. Excel files may be on different URL paths.
MI.launch_date_notes: Online sports betting and iGaming launched January 22, 2021.
MI.file_formats_notes: Excel files available for each year (2021-2026). URL structure not fully confirmed but follows MGCB media path pattern.
MI.archive_depth: Since January 2021 launch. Excel files for each year.
MI.requires_js_notes: Page requires JS to render. Direct file downloads also returned 404 (URL path may have changed). Playwright needed.
operators: BetRivers (Little Traverse Bay Bands of Odawa Indians)
PointsBet/Fanatics (Lac Vieux Desert Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians)
WynnBET (Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians)
FireKeepers (Nottawaseppi Huron Band of the Potawatomi)
Four Winds (Pokagon Band of Potawatomi Indians)
Hard Rock Bet (Keweenaw Bay Indian Community)
MI.operator_notes: 15 commercial and tribal operators authorized for iGaming and/or online sports betting. 12 currently offer online sports betting, all 15 offer iGaming. Unique tribal-commercial partnership model. Hard Rock Bet launched December 2, 2025. Operators file available as Excel download.
MI.sport_breakdown_notes: MI reports do not appear to break down by sport.
MI.retail_online_split_notes: MGCB reports internet sports betting separately from Detroit casino retail operations.

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Sports Betting Receipts
data_fields.description: Gross receipts minus free play/promotional deductions

data_fields.name: State Wagering Tax
data_fields.description: 8.4% tax on adjusted gross receipts

data_fields.name: Gross Receipts
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered/won before deductions

data_fields.name: Free Play Deductions
data_fields.description: Value of promotional/free play credits deducted

MI.data_fields_notes: Exact column headers need verification from actual Excel download. Fields based on MGCB press releases and regulatory filings. Monthly Excel files organized by operator.
MI.ggr_definition: Qualified Adjusted Gross Receipts = Gross Sports Betting Receipts minus the monetary value of free play provided to and wagered by patrons as an incentive or result of sports betting wagers.
MI.ggr_formula: Adjusted Gross Receipts = Gross Receipts - Free Play Deductions
MI.promo_deduction: Yes, fully deductible. Free play / promotional credits are deducted from gross receipts before tax calculation. This is a major policy point - Governor Whitmer has proposed ending unlimited promotional deductions (expected to add $21.1M to state budget).
MI.tax_basis: Adjusted Gross Sports Betting Receipts (after promotional deductions)
MI.tax_notes: 8.4% is among the lowest sports betting tax rates in the US (only Iowa and Nevada are lower at 6.75%). Governor has proposed increasing this. Unlimited promo deductions further reduce effective tax yield. Combined iGaming + sports betting generated $3.8B in 2025.
MI.scraping_approach: Excel files for each year need to be downloaded. URL pattern involves MGCB media paths that may change. Use Playwright to navigate to the revenue page, identify and click download links for Excel files. Files are in .xlsx format which is ideal for parsing. Tribal operator data may require separate handling.
MI.anti_scraping: michigan.gov returns 403 on direct WebFetch and some curl requests. Playwright browser automation works. Excel files may need authenticated download or browser session.
MI.sample_files_notes: Authorized operators Excel file downloaded (16KB). Revenue Excel file returned 404 (URL path may have changed). Revenue file for 2025 returned 73KB error page.
MI.notes: MI has unique tribal-commercial partnership model. 8.4% tax rate is very operator-friendly. Unlimited promo deductions significantly reduce effective state revenue. Governor pushing for tax reform. $435.9M internet sports betting AGR in 2025. FanDuel, BetMGM, DraftKings dominate. Hard Rock Bet launched Dec 2025 as newest entrant.
MI.log: WebFetch returned 403. WebSearch provided operator/tax details. Playwright screenshot captured successfully. Authorized operators Excel downloaded. Revenue Excel file download failed (404 - URL path may have changed).

key_findings: PA is the easiest state to scrape: direct Excel downloads, clear URL patterns, no anti-scraping measures
OH provides the most granular revenue decomposition with full formula transparency
NJ provides the richest overall dataset: operator-level, sport-level, channel-level, tax-level breakdowns all in one PDF
NY blocks direct file downloads requiring Playwright browser automation
IL offers CSV format which is ideal for parsing but page requires JS to access download links
MI has the lowest effective tax rate due to 8.4% rate + unlimited promo deductions
NJ is the only Tier 1 state that breaks down revenue by sport category
All 6 states break down by operator
5 of 6 states split retail vs online (NY mobile-only under this license structure)
Tax rates range from 8.4% (MI) to 51% (NY online)
